title: Source Reconciliation — AIS vs Prefill JSON
description: Compare the Annual Information Statement (AIS) against the ITR Prefill JSON for each overlapping income field. The Prefill JSON is generated by the IT Department from the previous year's data and the current year's 26AS snapshot at the time of generation. AIS is always more current than Prefill. Where AIS and Prefill disagree, AIS takes precedence. Stale Prefill data is a common cause of incorrect pre-filled returns.

logic.if: AIS is loaded (E-001) AND Prefill JSON is loaded (E-003)
logic.then: for each field present in both AIS and Prefill: compare amounts; if delta < 0.01 → reconciled; if 0.01 ≤ delta ≤ 0.05 → minor_variance (note: Prefill may be stale; proceed with AIS value); if delta > 0.05 → major_variance (flag Prefill field as stale_prefill; use AIS value; log conflict); if field in AIS but not in Prefill → acceptable_absent (Prefill simply did not include it; no action beyond noting); if field in Prefill but not in AIS → investigate_further (Prefill may reference prior-year data or IT Dept error; do not use Prefill value unless confirmed by user)
logic.else: if Prefill JSON not loaded: acceptable_absent — Prefill is not a required document; reduce ECS by 5 points; proceed without it. if AIS not loaded: R-0007 already handled this case; do not double-penalise ECS

notes: The Prefill JSON is populated at the time the taxpayer opens their ITR on the IT Portal. If the taxpayer waits until July to file, the Prefill may be 3-4 months old relative to the final AIS. AIS is updated by the IT Department continuously, so a July AIS is more accurate than a March Prefill. Never trust Prefill over AIS without confirmation.
